panels.title: Puzzle games — things to investigate
panels.content:
## Game state

Puzzle games often have clearly separated states: the menu or world map, an active puzzle, a completion fanfare, and any tutorial or story segments. A state index that changes across these transitions is worth finding early.

## Board and grid state

The current state of a puzzle board is usually a byte array — one byte per cell, encoding the piece type or empty state. Searching for this will return many results initially. Making exactly one move and filtering for values that changed is the most direct way to narrow it down.

## Move counters

A move counter that increments exactly once per player action is one of the more reliable values in puzzle games. It provides a clean anchor for efficiency-based challenge achievements.

## Timers

Timers in timed puzzle stages may be a float, frame counter, integer in hex, BCD, centiseconds in a single address, or split across separate addresses for minutes, seconds, and centiseconds. Search for a changing value during an active timed stage and investigate each format.

## Combo and chain tracking

In match-3 or falling-block games, chain and combo counters only increment during an active cascade. Letting the cascade fully resolve before reading the value produces more reliable results.

## Clears per character

If the game has character-specific modes, difficulty characters, or story routes per character, each may have its own completion record.

panels.title: Protection
panels.infobox: Identify these before writing achievement logic.

entry_Protection.name: Demo mode
entry_Protection.tNote: Check for a specific value that is set or cleared only on the main menu or when demo mode initiates.

entry_Protection.name: Save protection
entry_Protection.tNote: Find values that match a loading state or last loaded in-game state so that you can confirm the player is in-game for at least one frame by checking Delta = in-game.

entry_Protection.name: Cheat protection
entry_Protection.tNote: Investigate old guides or walkthroughs for possible cheats. If they offer an advantage, block them. Look for on/off flags, unnatural resource increases during a menu or pause scenario.

entry_Protection.name: Unlimited moves cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this exists, move-count achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: Unlimited time cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this exists, timed achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: Unlimited hints cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this exists, no-hint achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: Skip puzzle cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this exists, completion achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: Solution display cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: Any feature that reveals the answer. Must be off for challenge achievements.

entry_Protection.name: 2-player mode active
entry_Protection.tNote: Competitive puzzle mode. Investigate whether this needs to be excluded from solo achievements.

panels.title: Progression
panels.infobox: Values that track forward movement through puzzle packs, worlds, and story.

entry_Progression.name: Game state / screen index
entry_Progression.tNote: A value that changes between the menu, world map, active puzzle, and completion screens.

entry_Progression.name: Puzzle pack / world ID
entry_Progression.tNote: Which puzzle pack or world is currently active.

entry_Progression.name: Puzzle / stage ID
entry_Progression.tNote: Which specific puzzle within the active pack is loaded.

entry_Progression.name: Puzzle completion flag
entry_Progression.tNote: A value that changes when the current puzzle is solved.

entry_Progression.name: Pack completion flag
entry_Progression.tNote: A value that changes when all puzzles in a pack are cleared.

entry_Progression.name: Story / event flags
entry_Progression.tNote: Flags for story events or tutorial triggers.

entry_Progression.name: Puzzle completion flags — pack 0
entry_Progression.tNote: Which puzzles in pack 0 have been permanently solved. May be a bitmask.

entry_Progression.name: Puzzle completion flags — pack 1
entry_Progression.tNote: Pack 1. Compare address offset from pack 0 to determine entry size.

entry_Progression.name: Puzzle completion flags — pack 2
entry_Progression.tNote: Continue the pattern for each additional pack.

entry_Progression.name: Star or medal per puzzle
entry_Progression.tNote: Performance rating for each puzzle. May be a numerical tier or a medal type.

entry_Progression.name: Total puzzles cleared (count)
entry_Progression.tNote: A running count of all puzzles completed.

entry_Progression.name: New puzzle unlock flag
entry_Progression.tNote: A value that changes when completing a puzzle unlocks a new one.

entry_Progression.name: Clears per character
entry_Progression.tNote: If the game has character-specific routes or difficulty modes, where per-character completion is stored.

entry_Progression.name: Game completion flag
entry_Progression.tNote: A value set when all puzzles are cleared or the true ending is reached.

panels.title: Collections
panels.infobox: Things the player owns or has unlocked that persist across sessions.

entry_Collection.name: Hint stock
entry_Collection.tNote: Number of hints held in reserve.

entry_Collection.name: Power-up inventory — type 0
entry_Collection.tNote: Quantity of the first power-up type.

entry_Collection.name: Power-up inventory — type 1
entry_Collection.tNote: Second type. Compare offset from type 0.

entry_Collection.name: In-game currency / coins
entry_Collection.tNote: Accumulated currency for purchasing hints or power-ups.

entry_Collection.name: Total stars (global)
entry_Collection.tNote: A running total across all packs.

entry_Collection.name: Bonus level unlock flags
entry_Collection.tNote: Whether hidden or bonus levels have been unlocked.

entry_Collection.name: Character unlock flags
entry_Collection.tNote: If the game has unlockable characters or modes.

entry_Collection.name: Gallery / extra content flags
entry_Collection.tNote: Bonus content unlocked through star or clear milestones.

entry_Collection.name: Special item obtained
entry_Collection.tNote: One-of-a-kind rewards earned for specific completions.

panels.title: Challenges
panels.infobox: Values for achievements with pass/fail conditions.

entry_Challenge.name: Move counter (current puzzle)
entry_Challenge.tNote: A count that increments once per player action. Resets on puzzle load.

entry_Challenge.name: Move par (target)
entry_Challenge.tNote: The target move count for a perfect solve, if one is defined.

entry_Challenge.name: Puzzle timer
entry_Challenge.tNote: May be a float, frame counter, integer in hex, BCD, centiseconds in a single address, or split across minutes, seconds, and centiseconds.

entry_Challenge.name: Best time (current puzzle)
entry_Challenge.tNote: The stored personal best for the active puzzle.

entry_Challenge.name: Score
entry_Challenge.tNote: May factor in speed, move count, and bonuses.

entry_Challenge.name: Board / grid state
entry_Challenge.tNote: The current state of the puzzle board. Make exactly one move and search for changed values.

entry_Challenge.name: Active piece ID
entry_Challenge.tNote: Which piece or element is currently selected or falling.

entry_Challenge.name: Combo / chain counter
entry_Challenge.tNote: Cascades or chains. The value may only be meaningful after a cascade fully resolves.

entry_Challenge.name: Hint used (this puzzle)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Whether a hint has been used on the current puzzle. May reset on load.

entry_Challenge.name: Hints used (total)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Total hints used across all puzzles, if tracked persistently.

entry_Challenge.name: Undo counter (this puzzle)
entry_Challenge.tNote: How many undos have been used on the current puzzle.

entry_Challenge.name: Error / mistake counter
entry_Challenge.tNote: A count of invalid moves or wrong answers, if the game tracks this.

entry_Challenge.name: Lives / attempts remaining
entry_Challenge.tNote: How many attempts remain, if the game has a limited-attempt system.

entry_Challenge.name: Clears per character
entry_Challenge.tNote: How many completions each character has, if character-specific tracking exists.
